preamble: The shift from the velar stop [k] to the alveolar fricative [s] is a long-term historical process resulting from palatalization and assibilation. It typically begins when [k] occurs before front vowels (like [i] or [e]), pulling the tongue forward into an affricate [ts] or [tʃ], which later simplifies into a fricative [s].

examples.note: The initial velar stop palatalized before the front vowel 'e', eventually softening to a sibilant.
The defining feature of 'Satem' languages is the fronting and assibilation of PIE palatovelars.
